description: A reproduction of the Norman Rockwell mid-century illustration 'Merry Christmas, Grandma!' posted by the Department of Labor as a Christmas greeting. The painted (not designed-graphic) scene shows a 1950s American family crowding through a front doorway laden with wrapped gifts: a man in a brown hat and coat and a woman in a teal coat carry presents, a boy in a red cap holds a wrapped box, a girl in a red outfit holds a gift, an excited beagle leaps up at center, and a small child in a red hooded snowsuit sits on the floor at right mid-yawn/cry holding a present with a bow. The setting is a warmly decorated home entry with a holly-trimmed door, evergreen wreath with red bow, red paper garland, and a braided rug; the palette is rich reds, greens, and warm browns. There is no on-image text, agency seal, slogan, or graphic overlay; the only marking is the inherent artwork. Because it is a straight reproduction of an existing fine-art painting used as a seasonal greeting rather than an agency-designed meme/poster, it is marked is_designed=false. The intent is a wholesome, nostalgic holiday message to the public (the caption/attribution appears only in the tweet text, not on the image).
notable_text: (none on image; tweet attributes it as 'Merry Christmas, Grandma!' by Norman Rockwell)
